steps.title: Find angle T in radians
steps.content:
Angle S = \(\frac{8\pi}{13}\) radians. Angle T is 3 times angle S:
$$T = 3 \times \frac{8\pi}{13} = \frac{24\pi}{13} \text{ radians}$$

steps.title: Convert angle T to degrees
steps.content:
To convert radians to degrees, multiply by \(\frac{180}{\pi}\):
$$T = \frac{24\pi}{13} \times \frac{180}{\pi} = \frac{24 \times 180}{13}$$
The \(\pi\) cancels. Factor 24 = 8 × 3:
$$T = \frac{8}{13} \times 180 \times 3$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
• A — \(\frac{8}{13}(90)(3)\): uses 90 instead of 180. The radian-to-degree factor is 180/π, not 90/π. Wrong.
  • B — \(\frac{8}{13}(180)(3)\): this is exactly \(\frac{8}{13} \times 180 \times 3\), matching our derivation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C — \(\frac{8}{13\pi}(90)(3)\): places π in the denominator instead of canceling it, and uses 90. Both wrong.
  • D — \(\frac{8\pi}{13}(180)(3)\): keeps π in the numerator; the π should cancel during conversion. Wrong.
